title: Phenomenon 21
wikitext:
{{Decay Theme}}{{Phenomenon Class|frequency=3|effectiveness=5|danger=3}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Phenomenon 21: "On and On"}} == Description == '''Phenomenon 21''', also known as '''On And On''', is a somewhat rare occurrence in which unusually repetitive yet expansive sub levels manifest for short periods of time before disappearing. While not inherently dangerous, since the sub levels in question do not usually contain any hostile entities, an unknown fate may befall those who wander inside unwittingly and fail to escape before the environment disconnects from the rest of the backrooms. The time between conception and disappearance of the sub levels seems random, with recorded cases ranging between a single day and multiple weeks. The longer the manifestation, the more wanderers will inevitably become trapped. Despite initially seeming similar to [[Phenomenon 99]], '''Phenomenon 21''' exhibits several key distinctions; it only manifests sub levels, and said sub levels are confirmed to have existed at some point, as opposed to being blips of inconsistency in the timeline of the database or memories of wanderers. {{Blurimage|file=Level_0.02_p.png|caption=A photo taken in Level 0.02, speculated to be an instance of Phenomenon 21.}} Structurally, the phenomenon manifests environments almost perfectly resembling a single room from their parent level repeated over and over. Several wanderers have attempted to escape through the drop ceiling of a [[Level 0|Lobby]]-esque manifestation, only to be met with another identical floor. The same is speculated to be true of all manifestations, infinitely in every direction. While certain aspects may vary between instances, each instance is known to have very few walls, yet an unyielding ceiling and floor; some kind of grid pattern, be it the ceiling, lights or even repeating puddles; and a distant haze of particles obscuring one's vision. Some wanderers have theorized that this last aspect may connect '''Phenomenon 21''' to [[The Decay]], signifying a fundamental error in the creation of new levels that instead results in unstable, temporary and repetitive husks. This is unconfirmed. There have been rumors of the exact same sub levels reoccurring through '''Phenomenon 21''' (albeit with different numbers), mostly as branches of [[Level 0]]. It is unknown if this is actually the case or if the manifested environments are so similar that they are indistinguishable from previous iterations. Like their environments, the entrances and exits of instance of '''Phenomenon 21''' are equally variable. Holes, doors, ladders, no-clipping and all other normal methods of entering new levels may lead here, making avoiding manifestations difficult. Some wanderers have even reported exits that used to lead to another level directing wanderers to a manifestation during its lifespan, after which the exit would either return to normal or vanish entirely along with the sublevel. If one believes they have stumbled into a manifestation and can still utilize the entrance they came in from, they should do so immediately and avoid wandering deeper inside, no matter how tempting it may be. Navigating manifestations of '''Phenomenon 21''' is near impossible since every direction is identical.{{Blurimage|file=and on-1.png|caption=A manifested zone reminiscent of Level 1.}}Once its time is up, all entrances to the sublevel in question either disappear entirely or redirect wanderers who interact with them to a completely different level. Since each instance also seems to be affected by [[Phenomenon 15]], the fate of those who are inside the manifestations after disconnection is unclear. It is widely believed that they simply cease to exist along with the sublevel. Considering the alternative is to be lost indefinitely, instant death would be a mercy. == Discovery == '''Phenomenon 21''' was initially proposed after [[M.E.G.|MEG]] officials launched an investigation into a series of elusive yet suspiciously similar sub levels of [[Level 0]] which continued to be debunked yet later rediscovered. After documentation, several other short-lived sub levels matching the phenomenon's description were later attributed to it. Notably, the frequency of reports of new instances of the phenomenon have increased over the past few years, shortening from every few months to every few weeks, prompting leaders to warn wanderers when and where a manifestation has occurred. Some wanderers have even suggested adding a PSA system to the database if manifestations continue to appear more often. [[Dullness]] has been speculated to be an instance of '''Phenomenon 21''', but its extended lifespan and varied architecture have thus far discouraged its categorization as such. Further research is required to determine if manifestations of '''Phenomenon 21''' have qualities discerning them from normal sub levels other than their structure, or if their appearance and disappearance can be anticipated. {{Collapse|num=3|initial='''> Open Author Information'''|final='''> Close Author Information'''|body= Page written by [[User:SlenderTales|SlenderTales]]
